/**
 * @brief Format performance metric data points for the monitoring system
 * 
 * Formats the metric name and label key-value pairs into a standardized monitoring data point string.
 * The generated format is: metric_name{label_key1="label_value1",label_key2="label_value2",...}
 *
 * Input/output example:
 *   Input: metric_name="http_requests_total", label_keys=["method","status"], label_values=["GET","200"], label_count=2, metric_output points to a sufficiently large buffer
 *   Output: On success, returns the length of the formatted string; metric_output becomes "http_requests_total{method=\"GET\",status=\"200\"}"; on failure, returns -1
 *
 * @param metric_name Metric name string, e.g., "http_requests_total"
 * @param label_keys Array of label keys, each element points to a label key string, e.g., ["method", "status"]
 * @param label_values Array of label values, each element points to a label value string corresponding to label_keys, e.g., ["GET", "200"]
 * @param label_count Number of label key-value pairs, i.e., the length of the label_keys and label_values arrays
 * @param metric_output Output buffer to store the formatted metric string; the caller must ensure the buffer is large enough
 * @param buffer_size Size of the metric_output buffer (including the terminating '\0')
 * 
 * @return On success, returns the length of the formatted string (excluding the '\0' terminator); on failure, returns -1
 *         Failure conditions include: metric_name is NULL, label_count < 0, label_count > 0 but label_keys or label_values is NULL,
 *         metric_output is NULL, buffer_size is 0, or an error occurs during formatting (including buffer overflow)
 */
int format_metric_point(const char* metric_name, const char** label_keys, const char** label_values, int label_count, char* metric_output, size_t buffer_size);

#endif

#ifndef MONITORING_SYSTEM_H
#define MONITORING_SYSTEM_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <time.h>

/**
 * @brief Format performance metric data points for the monitoring system
 * 
 * Formats the metric name and label key-value pairs into a standardized monitoring data point string.
 * The generated format is: metric_name{label_key1="label_value1",label_key2="label_value2",...}
 *
 * Input/output example:
 *   Input: metric_name="http_requests_total", label_keys=["method","status"], label_values=["GET","200"], label_count=2, metric_output points to a sufficiently large buffer
 *   Output: On success, returns the length of the formatted string; metric_output becomes "http_requests_total{method=\"GET\",status=\"200\"}"; on failure, returns -1
 *
 * @param metric_name Metric name string, e.g., "http_requests_total"
 * @param label_keys Array of label keys, each element points to a label key string, e.g., ["method", "status"]
 * @param label_values Array of label values, each element points to a label value string corresponding to label_keys, e.g., ["GET", "200"]
 * @param label_count Number of label key-value pairs, i.e., the length of the label_keys and label_values arrays
 * @param metric_output Output buffer to store the formatted metric string; the caller must ensure the buffer is large enough
 * @param buffer_size Size of the metric_output buffer (in bytes), including space for the '\0' terminator
 * 
 * @return On success, returns the length of the formatted string (excluding the '\0' terminator); on failure, returns -1
 *         Failure conditions include: metric_name is NULL, label_count > 0 but label_keys or label_values is NULL,
 *         metric_output is NULL, buffer_size is 0, or an error occurs during formatting (such as buffer truncation)
 */
int format_metric_point(char* metric_name, char** label_keys, char** label_values, int label_count, char* metric_output, size_t buffer_size);

#endif
